venue: Frost Bank Center, San Antonio, TX

official_gamebook_evidence_summary.source_type: NBA Official Scorer's Report / Final Box + Play-by-Play
key_lines: Final: San Antonio Spurs 126, Minnesota Timberwolves 97.
Spurs won the 4th quarter 35-24 and expanded the biggest lead to 30.
San Antonio shot 52.8% FG, 34.4% from three, and 77.8% FT.
Minnesota shot 38.6% FG, 27.3% from three, and 80.0% FT.
Points in the paint: Spurs 68, Timberwolves 36.
Julius Randle final line: 36:23, 17 points, 10 rebounds, 1 assist.
Victor Wembanyama final line: 32:38, 27 points, 17 rebounds, 5 assists, 3 blocks.
Dylan Harper produced 12 points and 10 rebounds off the bench.
Keldon Johnson produced 21 points on 8-of-11 shooting.

team_totals.SAS: 114.5 market consensus; Kalshi YES taken at -112 on over 114.5
team_totals.MIN: 104.5 broader market pregame; live entry later taken at 102.5

lineup_status.DeAaron_Fox_status: Upgraded to good-to-go pregame after ankle question mark; played 34:29.

series_context: Game 5 was tied 2-2 entering the night after Minnesota won Game 4 114-109 while Wembanyama was ejected early.
Wembanyama was available for Game 5 with no further discipline and immediately re-established interior control.

locked_positions.closing_observed: Approximately -116 to -127 at 114.5 depending snapshot; DraftKings moved to 113.5 -129 on over.

model_reasoning_snapshot.pregame_read: Spurs offensive profile with Wembanyama active, Fox confirmed, and strong home response spot supported 115+ outcome path.
model_reasoning_snapshot.liveflow_validation: At 4:46 4Q, SAS led 110-91 and needed only five more points; model read was hold with strong hit probability. Keldon Johnson three at 3:09 pushed SAS to 115 and secured the over.

locked_positions.closing_observed: Kalshi +119 at 6.5 equivalent / 7+ ladder pricing; market held near entry.

model_reasoning_snapshot.pregame_read: Randle rebounding path was supported by projected 35+ minutes, series physicality, missed-shot volume, and Minnesota needing frontcourt possessions against Wembanyama/Gobert configurations.
model_reasoning_snapshot.liveflow_validation: Randle had 5 rebounds through 3Q, then grabbed 5 more in the 4Q, including multiple offensive rebounds during Minnesota's comeback/gap-closing attempt.

model_reasoning_snapshot.entry_rationale: Minnesota needed 56 second-half points to clear 102.5. The bet leaned on comeback pressure, Edwards/Randle minutes, and potential foul-extension path.
model_reasoning_snapshot.failure_reason: The blowout expanded, Minnesota shot only 36.4% FG in the second half and 23.5% from three, and late garbage-time substitutions capped the primary scoring path.

betting_result_summary.micro_bankroll_note: Micro-level bankroll live again; session finished positive despite one late LiveFlow miss.

sas_team_total_over_114_5.diagnostic: Strong model-market alignment. Pregame team-total over captured the true Spurs ceiling once Wembanyama control and paint pressure stabilized.

randle_7_plus_rebounds.diagnostic: Correct ladder target. Early foul/minute compression did not kill the rebound path because Randle played 36:23 and the 4Q created extra rebound volume.

min_team_total_over_102_5_live.diagnostic: Late live over failed because comeback scoring path was overestimated after the game tilted into a 20+ point margin and Minnesota's primary group lost efficiency.

q1.read: Fast/high-efficiency start. Wembanyama erupted for 18 points and 6 rebounds in Q1, immediately validating the Spurs response spot and SAS TT over profile.

q2.read: Minnesota offensive collapse quarter. Spurs defense held MIN to 25% FG in Q2 and expanded the halftime lead to 12.

q3.read: Minnesota briefly tied the game at 61, but SAS stabilized with bench scoring from Castle, Harper, and Keldon Johnson. Spurs entered Q4 up 18.

q4.read: Spurs closed through both starters and bench. SAS reached 115 at 3:09 on a Keldon Johnson shot, then extended garbage-time scoring to 126. Minnesota did not reach the live team-total threshold.

patch_queue_updates.trigger: Do not add live team-total over when target team trails by 15+ after halftime or 18+ after 3Q unless the line is at least 7 points below projected median and starters are confirmed to remain active.
patch_queue_updates.reason: MIN TT over 102.5 failed despite reasonable raw math because blowout/starter-pull risk and 2H efficiency decline were underweighted.

patch_queue_updates.trigger: When elite big returns after ejection/absence and team is at home in a tied playoff series, boost team total mean if usage, FT pressure, and paint creation show early confirmation.
patch_queue_updates.reason: Wembanyama posted 18/6 in Q1 and Spurs finished with 68 paint points; this was a direct team-total over driver.

patch_queue_updates.trigger: Maintain rebound ladder confidence for high-minute forwards even if early rebounds are low, provided minutes remain 34+ and opponent rim contests generate miss/putback volume.
patch_queue_updates.reason: Randle had only 5 boards through 3Q but finished with 10 due to 4Q offensive rebounding and continued minutes.

rolling_window_use: High-weight Game 6 input. Spurs paint control, Wembanyama response profile, and bench scoring tail are elevated. Minnesota live overs need stricter blowout and starter-extension filters. Randle rebound ladders remain playable only when projected minutes are secure and price stays plus-money.
